result:
Spaghetti = no boundaries. Fix:

**1. Name things right.** `processData()` lies. `parseCsvToUsers()` tell truth. Function name = contract.

**2. One job per function.** Does X *and* Y? Split. 20 lines max rule of thumb.

**3. Pure > stateful.** Input → output. No hidden mutation. Easier trace.

**4. Kill deep nesting.** Early return. Guard clauses. Flat > pyramid.
```js
// bad
if (user) { if (user.active) { if (user.perms) { ... } } }
// good
if (!user) return;
if (!user.active) return;
if (!user.perms) return;
...
```

**5. Group by feature, not type.** `/auth/{routes,model,service}` > `/routes/`, `/models/`, `/services/` scattered.

**6. Layer.** UI → service → data. One-way deps. No circular imports.

**7. Types/interfaces.** Even in dynamic langs. Shape = documentation.

**8. Delete dead code.** Unused = confusion. Git remember.

**9. Comment *why*, not *what*.** Code say what. Comment say why weird choice exist.

**10. Refactor when pain hit.** Rule of three: copy twice, abstract third time. Not sooner (premature abstraction = worse spaghetti).

Start: pick worst file. Extract 2-3 functions. Rename. Ship. Repeat.
